Table of contents

  • Overview
  • Questions
  • Data

Malformation Analysis

Author

Ryan Taylor

Overview

This document consolidates our work on a Bayesian hierarchical model using a soap-film smoother to describe the patterns of volume abnormalities in children with craniosynostosis.

Questions

Data

Data were sent by collaborators at Colorado (primarily Antonio Porras, Alejandro Cruz Guerrero, and Connor Elkhill).

Covariates are stored in a “merged_covariates” csv file that Josh Lukemire created from these data. Each image’s malformation map is stored as a \(500 \times 500\) matrix in its own RData file saved in a data folder in Josh’s OneDrive.

We clean the covariate data set for easier analysis and to identify rare subsets of the data that should be excluded from our early work.

Code 
# Set directory for references
here::i_am("2025.08_Malformation_Analysis.qmd")

# Set data folder
DATA_F <- here::here("../../data/Lukemire, Joshua's files - data/")

# Set subfolder with image data
MAP_F <- paste0(DATA_F, "unsigned_distances/")

# List all files with any extension (so no folders)
data_files <- list.files(MAP_F, pattern = "\\.")

# Read in covariates data
cranio_raw <- read_csv(paste0(DATA_F, "merged_covariates.csv"))

# Establish constanta

MAX_AGE <- 365

First, we ensure that all images in the covariate data are represented in the data folder and vice versa.

Code 
### Clean cranio covariates file

## Create new data set with easier names
cranio <- cranio_raw %>% 
  clean_names()

## Check if files in data were found in data folder
cranio %<>%
  mutate(
    fname = paste0(id_fmt, "_desc-unsigneddist.RData"),
    fname_exists = if_else(fname %in% data_files, 1, 0)
  )

# Print a table to check we have all files
fname_summ <- tibble(
  in_data_not_folder = sum(1 - cranio$fname_exists),
  in_folder_not_data = sum(!data_files %in% cranio$fname)
)

kbl(fname_summ, caption = "Check Files vs. Data Availability") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)
Check Files vs. Data Availability
in_data_not_folder in_folder_not_data
0 0

Next, we create a cleaner categorization of each image’s suture fusions. Information about the suture fusions for each image are initially stored in a series of binary columns: metopic_fused, sagittal_fused, left_coronal_fused, right_coronal_fused, left_lambdoid_fused, right_lambdoid_fused. We combine these into one column and create categories for “Bicoronal” and “Multiple.”

Code 
#| label: clean sutures

## Classify sutures

# Currently stored in binary columns with names ending in "fused"
# Reshape data to identify all combinations of sutures
fusion_df <- cranio %>% 
  select(id_fmt, matches("fused")) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = -id_fmt) %>% 
  mutate(
    name = name %>% 
      str_remove("_fused") %>% 
      str_replace_all("_", " ") %>% 
      str_to_title()
  ) %>% 
  mutate(fusion = if_else(value == 1, name, NA_character_)) %>% 
  group_by(id_fmt) %>% 
  summarize(num_fusions = sum(value, na.rm = T),
            fusions = paste(na.omit(fusion), collapse = "/")) %>% 
  ungroup()

# (1) count number of sutures
# (2) identify bicoronal (the only useful instance of multiple sutures)
# (3) identify other instances of multiple sutures
# (3) assign remaining suture types
cranio %<>%
  left_join(fusion_df) %>% 
  mutate(fusion_type = case_when(
    num_fusions == 0 ~ "None",
    fusions == "Left Coronal/Right Coronal" ~ "Bicoronal",
    num_fusions > 1 ~ "Multiple",
    T ~ fusions
  )) %>% 
  mutate(fusion_type = fct_infreq(fusion_type))

# Summarize frequencies of each suture type
fusion_summ <- cranio %>% 
  group_by(fusion_type) %>% 
  summarize(pics = n(),
            patients = n_distinct(id_base)) %>% 
  arrange(desc(pics)) %>% 
  mutate(pics_pct = pics / sum(pics),
         patients_pct = patients / sum(patients)) %>% 
  adorn_totals("row")

kbl(fusion_summ, caption = "Suture Fusions: Full Data") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)
Suture Fusions: Full Data
fusion_type pics patients pics_pct patients_pct
None 1718 1718 0.6542270 0.6972403
Sagittal 506 419 0.1926885 0.1700487
Metopic 155 118 0.0590251 0.0478896
Multiple 81 71 0.0308454 0.0288149
Right Coronal 65 53 0.0247525 0.0215097
Bicoronal 47 40 0.0178979 0.0162338
Left Coronal 37 36 0.0140899 0.0146104
Left Lambdoid 10 6 0.0038081 0.0024351
Right Lambdoid 7 3 0.0026657 0.0012175
Total 2626 2464 1.0000000 1.0000000

Next, we look at the availability of longitudinal data. No longitudinal data exist for patients with no suture fusions. Patients with suture fusions have an average of about 1.5 visits per person, and this average is fairly consistent even when we limit to the number of visits taken before the 1 year mark.

Code 
# Identify which visit each image is from and how many visits they had
visits_summ <- cranio %>%
  arrange(id_base, age) %>% 
  group_by(id_base) %>% 
  mutate(visit_num = 1:n()) %>% 
  # Count visits total and visits under age limit (currently 1 year)
  mutate(visits = max(visit_num),
         visits_max_age = max(visit_num[age <= MAX_AGE], na.rm = T)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  # Summarize visits on average and max by fusion group
  group_by(fusion_type) %>% 
  summarize(patients = n_distinct(id_base),
            pics = n(),
            visits_avg = mean(visits),
            visits_max = max(visits),
            visits_avg_max_age = mean(if_else(is.infinite(visits_max_age),
                                              NA_real_,
                                              visits_max_age), 
                                      na.rm = T),
            visits_max_max_age = max(visits_max_age, na.rm = T)) %>% 
  arrange(desc(visits_avg))
  
kbl(visits_summ, caption = "Longitudinal Visits: Full Data") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)
Longitudinal Visits: Full Data
fusion_type patients pics visits_avg visits_max visits_avg_max_age visits_max_max_age
Right Lambdoid 3 7 2.714286 3 1.428571 2
Left Lambdoid 6 10 2.000000 3 1.285714 2
Metopic 118 155 1.593548 3 1.452555 3
Right Coronal 53 65 1.523077 4 1.561403 4
Sagittal 419 506 1.426878 5 1.354545 5
Bicoronal 40 47 1.425532 4 1.166667 2
Multiple 71 81 1.296296 3 1.339286 3
Left Coronal 36 37 1.054054 2 1.062500 2
None 1718 1718 1.000000 1 1.000000 1

Next, we consider exclusion criteria.

First, there are a small number of patients who were labeled by the collaborators as “Craniosynostosis,” but have no suture fusions. Close inspection reveals that these are 6 of the 7 patients in the data with “sphenoidal involvement.” Therefore, we exclude these 7 images.

Second, it is worth investigating the category of “Multiple” fusions. We find that other than the “bicoronal” combination of fusions (left coronal and right coronal fusions but nothing else), no other combination has a sample size of more than 14. Therefore, we exclude 81 patients with “Multiple” fusions.

Third, we likewise find that Right Lambdoid and Left Lambdoid suture fusions are very rare in our data, with 10 and 7 images respectively. Therefore, we exclude lambdoid suture fusions.

Fourth, we find that 18 images in the data are associated with a “previous surgery.” Since we are more interested in the patterns associated with suture fusions before surgical intervention, we exclude these images. Notably, 6 of these 18 images have multiple suture fusions, so they would already be excluded.

Fifth, the median age in our data is 340 days, but the maximum is 3,695 days. We have found in previous work that brain growth is approximately linear over the first year, but slows down after that. To keep a consistent trend in our data, we exclude patients above 1 year of age.

Code 
#| label: exclusion criteria

## Investigate any issues with fusion type
# Sanity check: fusion category against collaborators' "group" column
fusion_group_summ <- tabyl(cranio, fusion_type, group)

kbl(fusion_group_summ, caption = "Collaborators' Group vs. Fusions") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)
Collaborators' Group vs. Fusions
fusion_type Craniosynostosis Normative
None 6 1712
Sagittal 506 0
Metopic 155 0
Multiple 81 0
Right Coronal 65 0
Bicoronal 47 0
Left Coronal 37 0
Left Lambdoid 10 0
Right Lambdoid 7 0
Code 
# Check the 6 images in "Craniosynostosis" group with no sutures
cranio_none <- cranio %>% 
  filter(group == "Craniosynostosis" & fusion_type == "None")

# These children all have sphenoidal involvement
cranio_sphen <- cranio %>% 
  group_by(fusion_type, group, sphenoidal_involvement) %>% 
  summarize(n = n()) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  arrange(desc(n))

kbl(cranio_sphen, caption = "Spehnoidal Involvement Summary") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)

Code 
## Investigate multiple sutures

fusion_multiple_summ <- cranio %>% 
  filter(fusion_type == "Multiple") %>% 
  group_by(num_fusions, fusions) %>% 
  summarize(pics = n(),
            patients = n_distinct(id_base)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  arrange(desc(pics))

kbl(fusion_multiple_summ, caption = "Summary of Multiple Fusions") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)

Code 
max_multiple_count <- max(fusion_multiple_summ$pics)

## Investigate previous surgery
cranio_surg <- cranio %>% 
  filter(previous_surgery == 1) %>% 
  group_by(fusion_type, group, sphenoidal_involvement) %>% 
  summarize(n = n()) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  arrange(desc(n)) %>% 
  adorn_totals("row")

kbl(cranio_surg, caption = "Images with Previous Surgery") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)
Images with Previous Surgery
fusion_type group sphenoidal_involvement n
Sagittal Craniosynostosis 0 8
Multiple Craniosynostosis 0 6
Bicoronal Craniosynostosis 0 2
Metopic Craniosynostosis 0 1
Left Coronal Craniosynostosis 0 1
Total - 0 18
Code 
### Create subset data
# Exclude rare fusion categories: multiple, lambdoid
# Exclude sphenoidal involvement (in Craniosynostosis group without suture)
# Exclude patients with previous surgery (10)
# Limit to max 1 year old
cranio_sub <- cranio %>% 
  filter(!fusion_type %in% c("Multiple", 
                             "Left Lambdoid", "Right Lambdoid") &
           sphenoidal_involvement %in% c(NA, 0) &
           previous_surgery %in% c(NA, 0) &
           age <= MAX_AGE) %>% 
  # Reduce number of columns
  select(id_base, fname, fusion_type,
         age, sex) %>% 
  arrange(id_base, age)

# Create version of data with only last image
cranio_sub_last <- cranio_sub %>% 
  group_by(id_base) %>% 
  slice_tail(n = 1) %>% 
  ungroup()

We generate a summary of the fusion types present in the remaining observations, to compare against the full data.

Code 
fusion_summ_sub <- cranio_sub %>% 
  group_by(fusion_type) %>% 
  summarize(pics = n(),
            patients = n_distinct(id_base)) %>% 
  arrange(desc(pics)) %>% 
  mutate(pics_pct = pics / sum(pics),
         patients_pct = patients / sum(patients)) %>% 
  adorn_totals("row")

kbl(fusion_summ_sub, caption = "Fusions in Subset Data") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)
Fusions in Subset Data
fusion_type pics patients pics_pct patients_pct
None 593 593 0.4640063 0.5055413
Sagittal 434 369 0.3395931 0.3145780
Metopic 130 105 0.1017214 0.0895141
Right Coronal 56 45 0.0438185 0.0383632
Bicoronal 34 31 0.0266041 0.0264280
Left Coronal 31 30 0.0242567 0.0255754
Total 1278 1173 1.0000000 1.0000000

Next, we load the malformation maps into one data set. Each malformation map is stored as a \(500 \times 500\) matrix of pointwise absolute value differences, and is saved in its own RData file. We load each RData file in the remaining subset of images selected above, and convert each matrix into a data.table object. Then, we combine these all into one data.table and reshape this object from wide to long. The resulting data set contains 250,000 rows for each image with one column for the row index in the matrix, one column for the column index in the matrix, and one column containing the pointwise difference for that matrix cell.

Code 
#|label: load maps

# Clear memory space

rm(list = setdiff(ls(), c("cranio_sub_last", "MAP_F"))); gc()
          used  (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb) limit (Mb) max used  (Mb)
Ncells 2794448 149.3    4925915 263.1         NA  4925915 263.1
Vcells 4946381  37.8   10146329  77.5      24576  7182731  54.8
Code 
# Save row number for merging later
cranio_sub_last %<>% mutate(image_num = 1:n())

# Load in all malformation maps
map_ls <- map(1:nrow(cranio_sub_last),
              function(z){
                load(paste0(MAP_F, cranio_sub_last$fname[z]))
                
                # Convert to data.table for quick manipulation
                w <- data.table(pointwise_diff)[
                  # Add file name
                  , image_num := z]
                
                if(z %% 50 == 0){ cat("On row", z, "\n") }
                
                return(w)
              })
On row 50 
On row 100 
On row 150 
On row 200 
On row 250 
On row 300 
On row 350 
On row 400 
On row 450 
On row 500 
On row 550 
On row 600 
On row 650 
On row 700 
On row 750 
On row 800 
On row 850 
On row 900 
On row 950 
On row 1000 
On row 1050 
On row 1100 
On row 1150
Code 
# Merge these into the same data table
map_dt <- rbindlist(map_ls)

rm(map_ls); gc()
            used   (Mb) gc trigger   (Mb) limit (Mb)  max used   (Mb)
Ncells   2830695  151.2    6330390  338.1         NA   6330390  338.1
Vcells 301586298 2301.0  622721105 4751.0      24576 596114879 4548.0
Code 
# Add row number for each image
map_dt[, row := as.numeric(1:.N), by = image_num]
                
# Reshape to long
map_dt <- melt(map_dt, id.vars = c("row", "image_num"),
               variable.name = "col",
               value.name = "diff",
               variable.factor = FALSE)

# Clean column indicator
map_dt[, col := as.numeric(substr(col, start = 2, stop = 4))]

# Bring image number back in front
setcolorder(map_dt, c("image_num", "row", "col", "diff"))

For easier analysis of these malformation maps, we then merge covariates onto this malformation map data set. Since the image data is large, this is a memory-intensive merge. To address this, we first trim the maps data down to the smallest rectangle that includes every non-missing pointwise difference. Next, we select very few covariates to merge. We keep the demographics age, sex, patient ID, and suture fusion category, and retain an image identifier to match with the covariates data.

Code 
# Identify extent of actual brain shape
map_range <- map_dt[, .(min_row = min(row[!is.na(diff)], na.rm = T),
                        max_row = max(row[!is.na(diff)], na.rm = T),
                        min_col = min(col[!is.na(diff)], na.rm = T),
                        max_col = max(col[!is.na(diff)], na.rm = T))]

# Filter data to this range of rows and columns
map_dt <- map_dt[
  row >= map_range$min_row & 
    row <= map_range$max_row &
    col >= map_range$min_col &
    col <= map_range$max_col
]

### Merge covariates to malformation maps

# Convert covariates to data.table
cranio_sub_last <- data.table(cranio_sub_last)

rm(); gc()
            used   (Mb) gc trigger    (Mb) limit (Mb)   max used    (Mb)
Ncells   2829022  151.1    6330390   338.1         NA    6330390   338.1
Vcells 273070963 2083.4 1632269524 12453.3      24576 2035087948 15526.5
Code 
# Left join cranio_sub_last onto map_dt;
# Create new columns in map_dt coming from cranio_sub_last ("i")
map_dt[cranio_sub_last, 
       on = "image_num",
       `:=`(id_base = i.id_base,
            age = i.age, 
            sex = i.sex,
            fusion_type = i.fusion_type)]

With this combined image and covariates data, we begin plotting malformation maps. First, we check whether all images have non-missing pointwise differences in the same matrix cells.

Code 
# Identify range of values for plots
diff_range <- c("min" = min(map_dt$diff, na.rm = T),
                "max" = max(map_dt$diff, na.rm = T))

### Understand where missing values are located

# Count non-missing values by row and column
map_locs <- map_dt[, .(non_na = sum(!is.na(diff))), by = .(row, col)]

# Plot non-missing values
plot_map_locs <- ggplot(map_locs) + 
  geom_raster(aes(x = row, y = col, fill = non_na)) +
  coord_fixed()

# Summarize non-missing values
kbl(tabyl(map_locs, non_na), caption = "Non-Missing Matrix Cells") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)
Non-Missing Matrix Cells
non_na n percent
0 12478 0.1911283
1173 52808 0.8088717

Next, we summarize the average differences by suture fusion and plot these averages on the brain shape.

Code 
#| label: map fusions

### Summarize differences by fusions

# Create dataset of average cell for each fusion
map_fusion <- map_dt[, .(diff = mean(diff)), by = .(fusion_type, row, col)]

# Plot brain map by fusion type
plot_fusion <- ggplot(map_fusion) +
  geom_raster(aes(x = row, y = col, fill = diff)) +
  facet_wrap(~fusion_type) +
  coord_fixed() +
  theme_void() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  labs(title = "Fusion Average Differences")

# Print fusion maps
plot_fusion

We add to this summary by showing how these averages change on average over time, dividing age into 6 bins of roughly 2-month windows of ages. We also compare these results to the sample sizes in each of these age-fusion bins.

Code 
### Investigate age

# Add 6 categories of age
map_dt[,
       age_bin := cut(age, breaks = seq(0, 366, 61), right = FALSE)]

# Take average pointwise differences in cell by fusion and age
map_fusion_age <- map_dt[, .(diff = mean(diff)), 
                         by = .(fusion_type, age_bin, row, col)]

# Plot brain map by fusion type and age
plot_fusion_age <- ggplot(map_fusion_age) +
  geom_raster(aes(x = row, y = col, fill = diff)) +
  facet_grid(fusion_type ~ age_bin,
             labeller = label_wrap_gen(width = 10)) +
  coord_fixed() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  theme_void() +
  theme(axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank()) +
  labs(title = "Fusion - Age Average Differences")

# Print fusion maps
plot_fusion_age
Code 
# Determine sample size for these averages

# Count images per fusion and age bin
size_fusion_age <- map_dt[
  , .(n = uniqueN(image_num)),
  by = .(fusion_type, age_bin)
]

# Reshape to match plot
size_fusion_age <- dcast(size_fusion_age, fusion_type ~ age_bin, 
                         value.var = "n")

# Print table
kbl(size_fusion_age, caption = "Fusion-Age Sample Size") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)
Fusion-Age Sample Size
fusion_type [0,61) [61,122) [122,183) [183,244) [244,305) [305,366)
None 131 134 104 70 72 82
Sagittal 66 136 90 36 23 18
Metopic 16 32 20 18 11 8
Right Coronal 5 16 9 6 6 3
Bicoronal 9 8 4 3 1 6
Left Coronal 5 7 8 6 2 2

Finally, we consider how each fusion type might differ between the two natal sexes recorded in the data.

Code 
# Take average pointwise differences in cell by fusion and sex
map_fusion_sex <- map_dt[, .(diff = mean(diff)), 
                         by = .(fusion_type, sex, row, col)]

# Plot brain map by fusion type and age
plot_fusion_sex <- ggplot(map_fusion_sex) +
  geom_raster(aes(x = row, y = col, fill = diff)) +
  facet_grid(fusion_type ~ sex,
             labeller = label_wrap_gen(width = 9)) +
  coord_fixed() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  theme_void() + 
  theme(axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank()) +
  labs(title = "Fusion-Sex Average Differences")

# Print fusion maps
plot_fusion_sex

For a preliminary model, we filter to the most frequent combination of fusion type, age range, and sex with craniosynostosis. This includes all images with Sagittal fusion between the ages of 61-121 days with sex = 1. Our model does not reflect the brain boundary and does not incorporate these other demographics. It is a generalized additive model with an intercept and a tensor product smooth of row and column indices.

Code 
rm(list = setdiff(ls(), c("cranio_sub_last", "map_dt", "map_range"))); gc()
            used   (Mb) gc trigger    (Mb) limit (Mb)   max used    (Mb)
Ncells   3012214  160.9    6330390   338.1         NA    6330390   338.1
Vcells 583268004 4450.0 1632269524 12453.3      24576 2035087948 15526.5
Code 
# Count images by fusion, age, and sex
size_fusion_age_sex <- map_dt[
  , .(n = uniqueN(image_num)),
  by = .(fusion_type, age_bin, sex)
][, dcast(.SD, fusion_type + sex ~ age_bin, value.var = "n", fill = 0)]

# Print before deleting to save memory
kbl(size_fusion_age_sex, caption = "Fusion-Sex-Age Sample Size")
Fusion-Sex-Age Sample Size
fusion_type sex [0,61) [61,122) [122,183) [183,244) [244,305) [305,366)
None 0 58 55 56 31 35 38
None 1 73 79 48 39 37 44
Sagittal 0 18 45 28 17 5 6
Sagittal 1 48 91 62 19 18 12
Metopic 0 6 10 5 7 1 5
Metopic 1 10 22 15 11 10 3
Right Coronal 0 3 9 3 4 5 2
Right Coronal 1 2 7 6 2 1 1
Bicoronal 0 6 6 2 2 0 4
Bicoronal 1 3 2 2 1 1 2
Left Coronal 0 3 6 6 5 2 2
Left Coronal 1 2 1 2 1 0 0
Code 
rm(size_fusion_age_sex); gc()
            used   (Mb) gc trigger    (Mb) limit (Mb)   max used    (Mb)
Ncells   3012394  160.9    6330390   338.1         NA    6330390   338.1
Vcells 583310441 4450.4 1632269524 12453.3      24576 2035087948 15526.5
Code 
# Run model on most common subset using coordinates
model_sagittal <- gam(diff ~ te(row, col), 
                      data = map_dt[fusion_type == "Sagittal" & 
                         age_bin == "[61,122)" &
                         sex == 1])

# Generate predictions from this model in a new dataset
new_dt <- get_predictions(model_sagittal, 
                          cond = list("row" = seq(from = map_range$min_row, 
                                                  to = map_range$max_row, 
                                                  length.out = 50),
                                      "col" = seq(from = map_range$min_col, 
                                                  to = map_range$max_col, 
                                                  length.out = 50)))
Summary:
    * row : numeric predictor; with 50 values ranging from 131.000000 to 364.000000. 
    * col : numeric predictor; with 50 values ranging from 121.000000 to 399.000000. 
    * NOTE : No random effects in the model to cancel.
Code 
# Plot these predictions (in a rectangle shape)
plot_model_sag <- ggplot(new_dt) +
  geom_raster(aes(x = row, y = col, fill = fit)) +
  coord_fixed() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  theme_void() +
  theme(axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank()) +
  labs(title = "Sagittal Model",
       fill = "Pred. Diff.")

# Plot the averages in this dataset to compare
plot_actual_sag <- ggplot(map_dt[fusion_type == "Sagittal" & 
                         age_bin == "[61,122)" &
                         sex == 1, 
                         .(diff = mean(diff)), 
                         by = .(row, col)]) +
  geom_raster(aes(x = row, y = col, fill = diff)) +
  coord_fixed() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  theme_void() +
  theme(axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank()) +
  labs(title = "Sagittal Average",
       fill = "Avg. Diff")

# Arrange these two plots side by side
ggarrange(plot_actual_sag, plot_model_sag)
